clause_text: Customer shall indemnify vendor for all third-party claims and vendor's total liability shall not exceed one month of fees.
reason: Customer-only indemnity and vendor liability cap below policy minimum created unacceptable legal risk.

clause_text: Each party's aggregate liability is capped at fees paid in the twelve months before the claim with carveouts for confidentiality, security breaches, infringement, and willful misconduct.
reason: Mutual twelve-month cap and expected carveouts matched the approved liability position.

clause_text: Vendor may use customer data, prompts, outputs, and support tickets to train and improve artificial intelligence models used across vendor products.
reason: AI training on customer data is prohibited unless legal approves explicit written language.

clause_text: Vendor may process customer data only to provide and secure the services and may create aggregated metrics that do not identify customer or individuals.
reason: Data usage was limited to service delivery and non-identifying aggregated metrics.

clause_text: Fees are invoiced annually in advance and due Net 45. Customer may dispute invoices in good faith.
reason: Annual prepayment required procurement approval even though invoice dispute rights were preserved.

clause_text: Invoices are due Net 30 after receipt of a valid invoice and customer may withhold disputed amounts in good faith.
reason: Payment terms matched policy and preserved invoice dispute rights.

clause_text: Vendor may suspend or terminate services immediately for suspected breach and customer must pay all fees through the end of the term.
reason: Vendor-only immediate suspension and accelerated payment obligations required legal review.

clause_text: Either party may terminate for uncured material breach after 30 days notice and vendor will delete or return customer data within 30 days after termination.
reason: Termination rights and post-termination data handling matched policy.

clause_text: The subscription renews automatically unless either party gives notice at least 90 days before renewal. Renewal fees may increase to vendor's then-current list price.
reason: Notice period exceeded 60 days and renewal pricing was uncapped.

clause_text: This agreement is governed by the laws of a non-preferred foreign jurisdiction and all disputes must be brought exclusively in vendor's local courts.
reason: Foreign governing law and exclusive vendor forum fell outside preferred jurisdictions.

clause_text: Vendor may use subprocessors listed online and will provide notice of material changes. Customer may object to new subprocessors that process personal data.
reason: Subprocessor language may be acceptable, but the record lacked the DPA and security exhibit needed for approval.

clause_text: The uploaded document contains a signature page and pricing table but no liability, data usage, termination, or renewal language.
reason: Key clauses were missing, so the reviewer could not confirm policy alignment.
